Title: Verify that vehicle markers are visible on the map without requiring any RBAC permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has NO specific map feature permissions configured - The live map is accessible - Multiple vehicles are active and tracked on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a minimal/no-RBAC role - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe whether vehicle markers are visible on the map canvas - 4- Verify no permission error or blank map is shown
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with minimal role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Vehicle markers are visible on the map canvas - 4- No RBAC restriction blocks the display of vehicle markers - markers show vehicle positions without PII

Title: Verify that vehicle markers display no PII on the map canvas regardless of the user's PII permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with full PII permission - Multiple vehicle markers are visible on the live map - No popup is active
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with full permissions including PII - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe all vehicle marker labels and overlays on the map canvas - 4- Verify whether any PII is visible directly on or adjacent to the vehicle markers
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed with vehicle markers - 3- Vehicle markers are visible - 4- No PII (driver name - contact - personal details) is shown directly on the map canvas for vehicle markers - markers display only vehicle icon or non-PII identifier

Title: Verify that No-Run passenger markers are visible on the map without requiring any RBAC permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has NO specific map feature permissions - No-Run passenger markers are present on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a minimal role - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe whether No-Run passenger markers are visible on the map canvas - 4- Verify no RBAC restriction blocks their display
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with minimal role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- No-Run passenger markers are visible as icon-only representations - 4- No RBAC check is required to view these markers - they render correctly

Title: Verify that No-Run passenger markers display only an icon on the map canvas and no PII
Preconditions: User is logged in with full PII permission - No-Run passenger markers are visible on the live map - No popup is active
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with full permissions - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe all No-Run passenger markers on the map canvas - 4- Verify whether any PII (name - address - contact) is displayed directly on or next to the No-Run marker
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- No-Run passenger markers are visible - 4- Markers display only an icon with no PII text rendered on the map canvas - PII is strictly restricted to hover/click popups only

Title: Verify that RBAC permission "PII display in map popups - Allowed" controls visibility of PII in hover/click popups
Preconditions: User is logged in as System Administrator - Two roles exist: Role A has "PII display in map popups" enabled - Role B has it disabled - A passenger marker is visible on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in as System Administrator and configure roles appropriately - 2- Log in as Role A - navigate to live map - hover/click on a passenger marker - observe popup content - 3- Log in as Role B - navigate to live map - hover/click on the same passenger marker - observe popup content
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Admin configuration is set correctly - 2- Role A: PII (passenger name - address) is visible in the hover/click popup - 3- Role B: PII is NOT visible in the popup - only non-PII content or a permission-denied placeholder is shown - RBAC controls PII display correctly

Title: Verify that RBAC permission "Driver messages display - Allowed" controls visibility of driver message contents in hover/click panels
Preconditions: User is logged in as System Administrator - Role A has "Driver messages display" permission enabled - Role B does not - A vehicle marker with associated driver messages exists on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in as System Administrator and configure driver message permissions per role - 2- Log in as Role A - navigate to live map - click on a vehicle marker - inspect hover/click panel for driver message content - 3- Log in as Role B - repeat the same steps - observe whether driver messages are visible
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Admin configuration is set correctly - 2- Role A: driver messages and message contents are visible in the click panel - 3- Role B: driver messages are NOT visible or shown only as a restricted/hidden section - RBAC controls driver message display correctly

Title: Verify that IDs in hover/click panels are rendered as clickable deep links only when "Deep-link clickability (view permissions) - Allowed" RBAC is granted
Preconditions: User is logged in with "Deep-link clickability" view permission enabled - A vehicle marker with associated Run - Trip and Client IDs is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with deep-link view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle marker to open the click panel - 4- Observe the Run - Trip - Vehicle and Client IDs in the panel - 5- Verify they are rendered as clickable hyperlinks
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click panel opens - 4- Run - Trip - Vehicle and Client IDs are displayed as clickable hyperlinks - 5- Clicking each link navigates to the respective detail page

Title: Verify that IDs in hover/click panels are rendered as plain non-clickable text when "Deep-link clickability (view permissions) - Allowed" RBAC is NOT granted
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that does NOT have "Deep-link clickability" view permission - A vehicle marker with associated Run - Trip and Client IDs is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role lacking deep-link view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle marker to open the click panel - 4- Observe the Run - Trip - Vehicle and Client IDs in the panel - 5- Attempt to click on an ID
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with restricted role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click panel opens - 4- Run - Trip - Vehicle and Client IDs are displayed as plain non-clickable text (no hyperlink styling or cursor pointer) - 5- Clicking the IDs does not navigate anywhere - no deep link action is triggered

Title: Verify that partial RBAC permission grants clickable links only for permitted entity types and plain text for restricted entity types
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has "view" permission for Trip and Vehicle but NOT for Run or Client - A vehicle marker with all entity IDs is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with partial deep-link permission (Trip and Vehicle only) - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle marker to open the click panel - 4- Observe each entity ID's rendering (Run - Trip - Vehicle - Client)
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click panel opens - 4- Trip ID and Vehicle ID are rendered as clickable hyperlinks - Run ID and Client ID are rendered as plain non-clickable text - RBAC is enforced per entity type

Title: Verify that vehicle markers remain visible on the map even when all RBAC-gated features (PII - messages - deep links) are disabled for the user
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has all RBAC-gated features disabled (no PII - no driver messages - no deep-link clickability) - Multiple vehicles are active on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with all RBAC-gated features disabled - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe whether vehicle markers are still displayed on the map canvas - 4- Verify the map is functional for basic vehicle visibility
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with fully restricted role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Vehicle markers are visible on the map canvas - 4- Map remains functional for basic vehicle tracking - no crash or blank map occurs due to restricted features

Title: Verify that No-Run passenger markers remain visible on the map even when all RBAC-gated features are disabled for the user
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has all RBAC-gated features disabled - No-Run passenger markers are present on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with all RBAC-gated features disabled - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe whether No-Run passenger markers are still visible - 4- Confirm icon-only display with no PII
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with fully restricted role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- No-Run passenger markers are visible as icon-only on the map canvas - 4- No PII or restricted content is displayed - markers are accessible without any RBAC gate

Title: Verify that removing "PII display in map popups - Allowed" permission at runtime immediately prevents PII display in subsequent popup interactions
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII popup permission active - User is on the live map - Administrator revokes the PII popup permission mid-session
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with PII popup permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a passenger marker and confirm PII is visible in popup - 4- In a separate admin session revoke "PII display in map popups - Allowed" for this user's role - 5- Without logging out open a new popup for another passenger marker - 6- Observe PII visibility
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with PII permission active - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- PII is visible in popup - 4- Permission is revoked by admin - 5- New popup is opened - 6- PII is NOT displayed in the new popup - permission enforcement is applied dynamically

Title: Verify that removing "Driver messages display - Allowed" permission at runtime immediately prevents driver message display in subsequent interactions
Preconditions: User is logged in with driver message display permission active - Administrator revokes the permission mid-session
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with driver message permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a vehicle marker and confirm driver messages are visible - 4- Admin revokes the "Driver messages display - Allowed" permission - 5- Click another vehicle marker without logging out - 6- Observe driver message visibility
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Driver messages visible in popup - 4- Permission is revoked - 5- New marker is clicked - 6- Driver messages are NOT displayed in subsequent panels - permission change is enforced immediately

Title: Verify that removing deep-link clickability permission at runtime immediately converts clickable IDs to plain text in subsequent popups
Preconditions: User is logged in with deep-link clickability permission active - Administrator revokes the permission mid-session
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with deep-link clickability permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a vehicle marker and confirm IDs are clickable links - 4- Admin revokes "Deep-link clickability (view permissions) - Allowed" - 5- Click another vehicle marker without logging out - 6- Observe the ID rendering
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- IDs are clickable deep links - 4- Permission is revoked - 5- New popup is opened - 6- IDs are rendered as plain non-clickable text - no deep link navigation is possible

Title: Verify that a user with only driver message permission can view messages but cannot see PII in the same popup
Preconditions: User is logged in with "Driver messages display - Allowed" enabled but "PII display in map popups - Allowed" disabled - A vehicle marker with both driver messages and PII-linked passenger data is accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with driver message permission only (no PII permission) - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a vehicle marker that has associated driver messages and passenger PII - 4- Observe the click panel content
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click panel opens - 4- Driver messages and message contents are visible - PII fields (passenger name - address etc.) are NOT visible - each RBAC permission applies independently

Title: Verify that a user with only PII permission can see PII in popups but cannot click deep links
Preconditions: User is logged in with "PII display in map popups - Allowed" enabled but "Deep-link clickability (view permissions) - Allowed" disabled - A passenger marker with PII data is visible on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with PII popup permission only (no deep-link clickability) - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a passenger marker to open the popup - 4- Observe PII visibility and the rendering of entity IDs
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click popup opens - 4- PII fields are visible in the popup - entity IDs are displayed as plain non-clickable text - deep-link navigation is not available - permissions are enforced independently per feature

Title: Verify that RBAC rules are consistently enforced across all map entity types (Run - Trip - Vehicle - Client) for deep-link clickability
Preconditions: User is logged in with deep-link view permission for Run only (not Trip - Vehicle or Client) - A vehicle marker associated with all entity types is visible on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with Run-only deep-link permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a vehicle marker that shows all entity IDs in the popup - 4- Verify clickability for each entity ID individually
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click popup opens with all entity IDs - 4- Run ID is a clickable deep link - Trip ID - Vehicle ID and Client ID are plain non-clickable text - RBAC is applied per entity type not per popup

Title: Verify that hover popup RBAC enforcement matches click popup RBAC enforcement for the same entity
Preconditions: User is logged in with a specific combination of RBAC permissions - A vehicle marker is visible on the live map - Both hover and click popup are tested for the same marker
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a specific RBAC combination (e.g. PII allowed - deep links not allowed) - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Hover over a vehicle marker and observe the popup content - 4- Click on the same marker and observe the click popup content - 5- Compare RBAC enforcement between both popup types
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Hover popup shows content consistent with RBAC (PII visible - IDs as plain text) - 4- Click popup shows the same RBAC-consistent content - 5- RBAC enforcement is identical between hover and click popup interactions for the same entity

Title: Verify that RBAC-restricted content does not appear in the page source or DOM when the user lacks permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with NO PII and NO driver message permissions - A passenger marker is clicked to open the popup - Browser developer tools are accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with no PII and no driver message permissions - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a passenger marker to open the popup - 4- Open browser developer tools and inspect the popup DOM elements - 5- Search for any PII text or driver message content in the DOM
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with restricted role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click popup opens without PII or driver message content - 4- Developer tools opened - 5- No PII text or driver message content is present in the popup DOM - server does not send restricted data to the browser for unauthorized users

Title: Verify RBAC enforcement is consistent across desktop and tablet screen sizes for all gated map features
Preconditions: User is logged in with a specific RBAC configuration - Application is tested on desktop and tablet viewports - Vehicle and passenger markers are visible on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in on a desktop browser with specific RBAC permissions (e.g. PII allowed - driver messages allowed - deep links not allowed) - 2- Navigate to the live map and open a click popup - observe PII - driver messages and deep link behavior - 3- Repeat on a tablet viewport - 4- Compare RBAC enforcement across both screen sizes
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Desktop view: PII visible - driver messages visible - IDs shown as plain text consistent with RBAC configuration - 2- Tablet view: same RBAC behavior enforced - popup adapts responsively - 3- RBAC enforcement is identical across desktop and tablet - no permission bypass occurs due to screen size differences
